evals.name: New endpoint returns through the envelope and raises AppError
evals.prompt: Add a GET /items/{item_id} endpoint to our FastAPI app. It looks up an item by id from the repo; if the item does not exist it should fail with a not-found error. Follow our API conventions.
evals.expected_output: A handler that returns through responses.ok(...) on success and raises AppError(ErrorCode.ITEM_NOT_FOUND, {"resource": "item"}) when missing — never HTTPException, never a raw dict.

evals.name: Register a new error code across enum, status map, and both locales
evals.prompt: We need a new error for when an order has already been shipped and can no longer be cancelled. Register the error code ORDER.ALREADY_SHIPPED (HTTP 409) following our conventions, including all the places it has to be kept in sync.
evals.expected_output: An ErrorCode member ORDER_ALREADY_SHIPPED = "ORDER.ALREADY_SHIPPED", a 409 entry in _HTTP_STATUS, an errors.order.already_shipped key in BOTH en.yaml and zh-TW.yaml, and a catalog entry.

assertions.instruction: Verify the new message_key errors.order.already_shipped is added to BOTH en.yaml AND zh-TW.yaml — the two locale tables must stay in sync.

evals.name: Review a handler that leaks PII and bypasses the envelope
evals.expected_output: Flags: (1) raises HTTPException instead of AppError, bypassing the envelope; (2) returns a raw dict instead of responses.ok(...), bypassing the envelope; (3) PII leak — email value in the error detail and email+message in the f-string log line. Recommends raise AppError with field NAMES only in params, log via extra= without PII, and return ok(...).

assertions.instruction: Verify the review flags BOTH the envelope bypass (HTTPException + raw dict return) AND the PII leak (email value in error detail and email/message interpolated into the log f-string), and recommends params carry field NAMES not values.
